# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.147.0/http/server.ts";

serve(async (req) => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  let modulePath = url.searchParams.get("module") || Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");

  if (!modulePath) {
    return new Response("Module path is required", { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    // Dynamically import the module
    const module = await import(modulePath);

    // Call a default function or export from the loaded module, assuming it exists
    if (typeof module.default === "function") {
      return new Response(await module.default(), { headers: { "content-type": "text/plain" } });
    } else {
      return new Response("Module does not have a default function", { status: 500 });
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Failed to load or execute the module:", error);
    return new Response("Failed to load or execute the module", { status: 500 });
  }
});
